item.title: Animations (Blender)
item.description: Animations are nothing more than the sequential rendering of multiple frames. These frames will display your set of objects from the point of view of a [[camera]] and will render, step by step, what these objects are doing through time.

item.title: Blender
item.description: Table of contents [[Workspaces in Blender]] [[Shortcuts]] [[Meshes]] [[Objects]] [[Keyframes]] [[Animations]] [[animating]] Shading [[Texture Painting]] [[UV Mapping]] Tools [[Graph Editor]] Rendering [[Supported GPUS]] [[Depth of Field]] [[Rendering workflow]]

item.title: Blender Marketplaces
item.description: In these places we can get/buy assets that are useful for building a Blender render: TurboSquid https://www.turbosquid.com/ Beware of conversions, the quality of the model is always better when you’re using the software that it was originally built for.

item.title: Depth of Field (Blender)
item.description: The Depth of Field for a rendering scene is, in short, where the focus of the camera is and how much more far away a scene can be rendered before it starts losing focus.

item.title: Graph Editor (Blender)
item.description: The graph editor can be selected from the Editor Selector. ![[notes/blender/Editor selector.png]] It allows for the graph-edition of properties to be animated across different frames.

item.title: Layout workspace (Blender)
item.description: The layout workspace is the workspace in [[Blender]] where you’d do most of the layout (positioning) work for the different elements that will be part of your final render.

item.title: Render on AWS
item.description: Create a p2.xlarge instance, which I found is the best balance between performance and price. Make sure it’s ubuntu based.

item.title: Supported GPUs
item.description: Up to Blender 2.70, these are the GPUs that are supported to be used with the Cycles / Eevee renderer.

item.title: Texture exporting
item.description: Different formats have different ways, but for [[OBJ]] and [[FBX]] exports (what I needed) with textures, not only you’ll need the file itself, but also the texture files (images, if any), and, alternatively, a material files that maps between the two of them.

item.title: Texture Painting
item.description: Texture painting is the practice of applying images or paint onto a texture image. To be able to texture paint, the following three things need to happen:

item.title: UV Mapping
item.description: UV Mapping is the process of mapping a 2D texture to use onto a 3D object. ![[notes/blender/UV Mapping on Globe.png]]

item.title: Workspaces in Blender
item.description: Workspaces are the different layouts in the Blender application that are setup to more efficiently work in different aspects of the [[Rendering workflow]].
